description: A cached JS TypedArray view of WASM memory is used after memory.grow detaches the old buffer, reading stale data or throwing.
body: Failure mode: JS creates a Uint8Array over memory.buffer and caches it, then a WASM call invokes memory.grow, which replaces the backing ArrayBuffer and detaches the old one, so the cached view reads stale bytes or throws on a detached buffer. Detection signals: intermittent wrong reads or 'detached ArrayBuffer' exceptions after operations that allocate; bugs that appear only once input is large enough to trigger growth. Do not cache a memory view across a call that can grow the heap. Safer fallback: re-create the view from memory.buffer after any call that may grow, or copy data out immediately, keep views short-lived, and verify with a test that grows memory between obtaining and using a view.
